content.problemStatement:
Chef has a random permutation P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ and an integer KKK.

A subsequence (not necessarily contiguous) of this permutation is called good if it has at most KKK pairs of neighbouring elements such that the left element is larger than the right element.

Chef wants to know the maximum length of a good subsequence of his permutation. Can you help him?
constraints: 1≤Pi≤N1 \le P_i \le N1≤Pi​≤N for each valid iii

the permutation is chosen uniformly randomly among all permutations of the integers 111 through NNN; note that KKK is not independent from the chosen permutation (it can be chosen differently depending on the permutation)
`ExplanationOne good subsequence with the maximum length is [2,3,4,1,5][2, 3, 4, 1, 5][2,3,4,1,5].
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum length of a good subsequence.
